source.title: Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus
source.author: Ludwig Wittgenstein
source.edition_layout: bilingual: German original + Ogden English translation interleaved

extraction.method: Python re-based LaTeX stripping (preamble/comments/macros). Imperfect; canonical source remains 5740-t.tex.

1.phrase: The world is everything that is the case.

2.phrase: What is the case, the fact, is the existence of atomic facts.

3.phrase: The logical picture of the facts is the thought.

4.phrase: The thought is the significant proposition.

5.phrase: Propositions are truth-functions of elementary propositions.

6.phrase: The general form of truth-function is

7.phrase: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
